content.problemStatement:
You have a tree with NNN nodes rooted at node 111. You want to color each node of the tree either red or blue.

A coloring of the tree is considered to be good if for **every** subtree, the absolute difference between the number of red and blue nodes in that subtree is at most 111.

Find the number of good colorings of the tree.

The count can be large, so print it modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN ‚Äî the number of nodes in the tree.
The next N‚àí1N-1N‚àí1 lines describe the edges. The iii-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers uiu_iui‚Äã and viv_ivi‚Äã, denoting an edge between uiu_iui‚Äã and viv_ivi‚Äã.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of good colorings of the tree, modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: The input graph describes a tree on NNN vertices.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 3‚ãÖ1053\cdot 10^53‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: There are only two good colorings, which are the ones where nodes 111 and 222 have different colors.
